excerpt: The Arabic word for 'beginning' is badā. When one reverses the letters of badā, the result is ādāb. Thus ādāb is understood as the heart of knowledge itself.
source_location_hint: On the essence of comportment and spiritual knowledge

excerpt: The Prophet asked, 'Where is your mercy?' making clear that one requires neither revelation nor divine law to comprehend basic human decency.
source_location_hint: Incident from the Battle of Khaybar

excerpt: Whoever among us does not show mercy to the young, and does not honor the great among us, is not truly of us.
Whoever practices what he has learned, Allah will grant him as an inheritance knowledge of what he did not know.
source_location_hint: On the relationship between knowledge and practice

excerpt: When My servants ask you about Me—indeed I am near. I respond to the call of the supplicant when he calls upon Me.
source_location_hint: On Allah's proximity and responsiveness to supplication

excerpt: All of its teachings derive directly from the sayings of Mawlānā ʿAlī as recorded in the Nahj al-Balāgha. All Sufis unanimously recognize Mawlānā ʿAlī as their spiritual leader.
source_author: ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib (compiled by al-Sharīf al-Rāḍī)
source_location_hint: On the foundations of Sufism and spiritual discipline

excerpt: Our Lord, do not let our hearts deviate after You have guided us.
source_location_hint: On prayer for the firmness of the heart

excerpt: The beginning of humility is to force oneself to be humble.
source_location_hint: On the cultivation of spiritual virtues

excerpt: The acquisition of knowledge is farḍ (obligatory) upon every Muslim man and woman.
source_location_hint: On the obligation to seek knowledge

excerpt: My aim is to share with you the underlying core principles of our ʿaqīda—first as Muslims, and then as Believers.
source_location_hint: On the distinction between Islam and Iman

excerpt: The disbeliever is bewildered by creation, whereas the believer is bewildered by the Creator.
source_location_hint: On the different orientations of the human soul
